title: pavonia striata
intro: Pavonia striata, also known as Rock Rose or Mexican Rose Mallow, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Malvaceae family. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Pavonia striata has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Pavonia striata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as salves or tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and therapeutic benefits.
